diagnosis: Visible/meta PDF grounding was added and tested on five Elsevier pdf_url candidates. Browserbase rendered the ScienceDirect pages, but the proposed /pdf URLs redirected to /abs pages and no rendered/meta PDF URL was discoverable in the captured HTML.
recommendation: Do not scale Elsevier pdf_url backfill until the lane can ground a real PDF-like URL from citation_pdf_url, visible PDF link, pdf.sciencedirectassets.com, or md5/pid pdfft evidence.

diagnosis: Relative DOI PDF links were already selected by the parser but joined against doi.org when cached pages had no canonical/og base URL. The fix maps already-selected relative /doi/pdf, /doi/epdf, and /doi/pdfdirect links to publisher hosts by DOI/path family, without inventing new PDF links.
recommendation: Ship this narrow parser fix. Remaining Taylor pdf_url gap is mixed: 21 gold-present/parser-empty rows have no reliable static PDF marker in cached HTML, 124 Goldie-empty/parser-present rows remain backfill candidates, and several divergent rows are classifier/gold/scorer owned.

diagnosis: Older Wiley pages with real abstract signals but no modern loa-authors block were bypassing the Wiley parser. The dispatcher then fell to generic/no output. Wiley now advertises authors_found when the page is Wiley-owned and has an abstract signal, allowing existing Wiley abstract fallbacks to supply abstract-only output while preserving empty/gold-backfill policy.
recommendation: Ship. Continue with the next ranked below-98 field; remaining Wiley abstract misses are mixed between gold truncation, cross-publisher Elsevier/Blackwell routing, Cochrane unsupported pages, and long-body manual-DOM cases.
ship

diagnosis: Oxford product pages outside academic.oup.com expose current-Goldie authors through schema.org author metadata while lacking at-ArticleAuthors DOM. Short cookie-reload and robot pages were previously scored as parser misses; they are now retrieval_blocked. Remaining Oxford author misses are manual-DOM/gold-owned cases.
recommendation: Ship. Oxford authors is above 98% on the scoped current-Goldie slice after schema-author fallback and honest cache-blocker accounting. Remaining misses are manual DOM/gold-owned or retrieval-blocked; continue next ranked below-98 publisher-field after report 336 publication.

diagnosis: SAGE abstract gap was mostly scorer-owned: current Goldie stores literal ellipsis-terminated abstract prefixes while Parseland emits the full page abstract. The scorer now credits only normalized prefix matches, preserving empty policy and real mismatch detection.
recommendation: Ship scorer fix. SAGE abstract improves but remains below 98%; residual rows are parser-empty SAGE Knowledge pages, retrieval-blocked rows, one backfill candidate, and non-truncated full-text mismatches for follow-up diagnosis.

diagnosis: Springer old-layout affiliation blocks list the same author under multiple affiliations; parse_authors_method_2 now aggregates those block-local affiliations per author and preserves comma-separated credential suffixes as part of the author name.
recommendation: Ship. Springer affiliations moves above 98% fuzzy F1 on the current 10K slice; remaining affiliation misses are small/manual or Goldie-backfilled candidates.

diagnosis: Wiley pdf_url residuals were mostly scorer-owned URL identity variants plus two parser-side front-matter PDFs hidden by the global .fmatter blacklist. Remaining misses are retrieval/cache/manual-DOM or gold/cross-publisher conflicts.
recommendation: Ship. Wiley pdf_url crosses 98% on the current 10K slice; remaining rows are retrieval/cache/manual-DOM or gold/cross-publisher conflicts rather than broad parser/scorer patterns.

diagnosis: Springer book/chapter pages expose the authoritative article/chapter byline in ul.c-article-author-list while legacy paths can return a partial byline, book editors, or no corporate author. The parser now uses that short byline as a conservative repair source.
recommendation: Ship. Springer authors crosses 98%; small fuzzy-affiliation tradeoff remains above 98% for Springer and is documented in the Shield artifact.

diagnosis: Wiley affiliation headroom was dominated by stale cached challenge/error pages being counted as parser misses, plus one parser-owned correspondence-address-only affiliation row. The evaluator now blocks obvious Just a moment, Cookies Turned Off, and unrelated FBI cached pages as retrieval_blocked; Wiley now extracts explicit author-affiliation blocks, preserves spaces across br-separated affiliations, and recovers postal correspondence addresses only as a fallback.
recommendation: Ship. Wiley affiliations crosses the 98% current-Goldie target; remaining Wiley affiliation tail is mixed parser/gold/cache/non-Wiley ownership and should be reranked after shipping this measured slice.

diagnosis: Wiley corresponding residual included legacy page patterns: single-token surname authors named only in article-header__correspondence-to, duplicated legacy Corresponding Author badges that overmarked every author, plain per-author Email:/correspondence lines, and bare author-info Corresponding author labels next to protected email links. Non-Wiley/generic residuals were left untouched.
recommendation: Ship. Wiley corresponding crosses the 98% current-Goldie target. Continue with Wiley authors or the next higher-ranked global cell after report 336 publication; keep Browserbase backfill blocked until the key is available through the environment, not pasted into command text.

diagnosis: Legacy TandF pages can expose author affiliations in div.author-infos Notes on contributors blocks instead of the author overlay. The Taylor parser now uses those structured author-bio blocks only when overlay affiliations are empty. Remaining Taylor-affiliation misses are mostly non-Taylor/classifier rows or TandF pages without visible structured author-bio affiliation evidence.
recommendation: Ship as a small measured current-Goldie lift. Taylor affiliations remains below 98%; remaining current-Goldie headroom should be classified as non-Taylor/classifier, no-visible-affiliation, or specialist/manual DOM rather than another broad Taylor overlay patch.

diagnosis: CUP fallback code existed but dispatch skipped older Cambridge pages because authors_found only accepted div.author. CUP now dispatches on Cambridge canonical/og metadata plus abstract/author meta signals, allowing citation_author/div.abstract fallback. Visible no-abstract notices are preferred over title-like og:description metadata when present.
recommendation: Ship. CUP abstract current-Goldie scored slice crosses 98%. The overall abstract ratio has a small accounting dip because 57 page-visible CUP abstracts/notices moved from empty-empty pass to Goldie-backfilled candidate status; this is correct lane separation, not a parser regression. Remaining CUP current-Goldie miss is 10.1017/9781108227049.007, whose cached page lacks the gold abstract block and should route to harvest/render/manual-DOM.

diagnosis: Cambridge book/chapter pages expose chapter authors in visible contributor rows, while the old CUP path selected editor/detail blocks or generic citation metadata. CUP now parses chapter contributor rows first, preserves matching detail affiliations, ignores non-author role prefixes, and opts into publisher-authors-over-generic priority only for CUP.
recommendation: Ship. CUP authors improves by +3.989pp and CUP affiliations crosses/stays above 98%; remaining CUP author misses are page-vs-Goldie disagreements or abbreviated/subset labels requiring Goldie-backfilled/gold-auditor review, not another parser patch.

diagnosis: Legacy ACS pages expose corresponding author only through Cloudflare-protected or mailto correspondence email links. The parser now decodes/reads those links and marks a unique author match, while parser-present/gold-empty rows stay in the Goldie-backfilled lane.
recommendation: Ship this small ACS corresponding lift now. Follow up separately on niwa.o last-dot-initial matching if ACS remains the next best publisher-local cell; remaining no-signal/invalid/page-mismatch rows are not safe parser wins.

diagnosis: Map ACS author-affiliation symbols (.author-aff-symbol) to div.aff-info span.aff-symbol before falling back to shared page-level affiliations. Residual ACS affiliation misses lack safe author-to-affiliation symbols or are C&EN/page-vs-gold cases.
recommendation: Ship the safe ACS symbol mapping. ACS affiliations remains just below 98% in full 10K (0.97735); remaining rows need gold/manual-DOM review or Browserbase grounding, not broad parser heuristics.

diagnosis: Two Lippincott-classified AHA PDF rows differed only by ahajournals.org ?download=true. Host-scoped scorer normalization fixed those rows. Remaining six failures require Browserbase/gold-auditor/manual-DOM rather than broad URL equivalence.

diagnosis: SSRN pages in the Elsevier-classified bucket render the contact author in per-author div.author blocks. The SSRN parser only checked the first div.author, so contact authors after the first author were parsed as non-corresponding. The patch builds contact-author name keys across all SSRN author blocks and matches them back to parsed author names by first initial plus surname, handling page middle initials that are absent from the parsed h2 name.
recommendation: Ship the SSRN contact-author parser fix. Elsevier corresponding remains below 98 after this lift; route the residual mix to manual DOM/gold-auditor/legacy portal specialist lanes rather than broadening SSRN or Elsevier heuristics without DOI-level evidence.
